AL IS THE BEST!!! No one compares

Al, that's what he'll want you to call him, is amazing! Truly everything you could ever dream of in a college prof. He honestly cares so much for his students' education. (Don't be afraid to ask for help/extensions)

Amazing course. Amazing experience. Amazing professor.

Al is passionate, knowledgable, encouraging and hip. Any student who has a class with him is extremely fortunate.

Attendance not mandatory, but I am still going to class.  Simply the best.
Al Filreis is an enthusiastic, open-minded, intelligent, and welcoming prof, who's passionate about his subject, about teaching, collaboration, and discussion. His is an amazing, eye-opening course.
great

Most excellent Mooc

modpo: greatest course ever taken... so far... until next year, when modpo's back!

this online class advances the absurd proposal that it is possible to teach a course on modern american poetry to a football stadium full of people in a pedagogically valid manner and then proceeds to accomplish just that. this course doesn't give you a fish, it teaches you how to fish.

Al is an inspirational teacher and I'm just one more of thousands who found ModPo enormously enriching and rewarding.  Thank you!
Al brought such an incredible amount of passion and interest to this course and really made the experience so enjoyable and emotional for me. He really is a wonderful teacher
This is it. Al is the best. Modpo is more than a course, it's an international poetry salon on a mission.

Al is one of a kind. I was amazed at the extent to which the course was an emotional experience and not merely an intellectual one. One  senses a personal relationship with Al and the TA's even if you are thousands of miles away on a different continent. I would   love to meet them.
Wonderful professor.  Spent inordinate amount of time teaching this course.  Learned more in this course than in any other humanities course I have ever taken.

I really enjoyed the video sessions by Prof Al Filreis. He had a unique style which made learning such fun and at the same time one think deeply over issues that were universal.He is an inspiring teacher with a natural flair for skilled classroom presentations.

I did not imagine that an online course in poetry could be so compelling and involving for students.  Al reached out through forums, videos, weekly call-in programs, etc to let every student be engaged in poetry.  His passion for modern poetry was contagious!

Al did what I would not have thought possible: created an online environment where he was able to convey his knowledge and his passion for poetry to a huge audience. He did it through enthusiasm and commitment. His level of engagement was incredible. Anywhere you went in the discussion forums, Al was there. He's STILL there; and so are we.

Professor Al Filreis is both passionate and well versed in poetry. Areas of study as subjective and often abstract as this one require just such a teacher. In the on-line course, I was impressed by his commitment to reaching out to the 30,000+ students. He constantly appeared on numerous threads numerous times. Al gave us a key to  all poetry.

Al is very involved in the day activities of the class.  He was involved daily in discussion board and our FB Modpo group discussions.  I found myself immersed in the material presented in the class and the lessons and insights gleaned from Modpo have carried over into my day to day life.

Al Filreis made the course of Modern and Contemporary American Poetry incredibly interesting. What is very valuable in the work of Professor Filreis is that he tries not to leave without the answer any question, what could seem impossible in MOOCs.

He helps one see poets and writers in a new light as if they had been partially hidden in chiaroscuro until then. He expounds his ideas in socratic fashion, so that one comes to understand what one otherwise may have missed about their worth. Most of all he helps one feel they can be part of what literature is all about.
Great course. Fabulous professor. Awesome TAs.

He provided a challenging and satisfying overview of modern and contemporary American poetry.  His use of a roundtable discussion rather than lecture format provided an high-interest learning environment.

I took the inaugural Coursera MOOC for UPenn's English 88 called Modern and Contemporary Poetry with students from all over the world.  Prof Filreis taught me how to read a poem and made the process relevant to my life to the point that I plan to be his student forever.  He is fantastic and demanding (and deserving) of one's best efforts.

Excellent class. Al Filreis is an excellent teacher who seems be able draw your interaction even though you are 3,000 miles away taking this online course. He has really added to my poetry experience. Take this online class (Modern&Cont Amer Poetry) through Coursera next Sept (2013).
His enthusiasm and interaction with the students MADE the class! What a wonderful, engaging, and terrific course this was on Modern and Contemporary Poetry!

Al Filreis is truly one of the finest teachers I have encountered at any level from kindergarten through graduate school: scholarly but down-to-earth, pedagogically expert, creative, witty, warm, congenial, helpful, generous of his time and spirit. He IS ModPo. I would truly take anything he teaches.
Fantastic. Best course on any subject I have ever taken, and that's a lot of courses.
Great professor and excellent class I took with him on Coursera. You will love any class with Al- he's friendly, engaging, and helpful.
Professor Al had been a great teacher throughout the course. Totally changed my level of understanding the poetry.

Great Professor, great experience to take his online class!

Al Filreis is an amazing teacher. He gives me hope that there are still inspirational teachers out of poetry and English left in this world. Al's Modern & Contemporary Poetry class is top-notch. One of the BEST college classes I've taken and I'm 40 years old!

Prof. Al is the best teacher I've had.

Kateis not the supportive, student-centered professor people describe. It often feels like she does not meet students where they are, and the expectations can seem unclear and discouraging. Instead of feeling supported, many students feel stressed and overwhelmed. I would carefully reconsider before taking her.
Kate is a highly ethical professor who cares a great deal about meeting students where they are and supporting their education. Take her for Advanced Clinical Practice.

The ethic of care means supporting students' learning, encouraging questions, and treating mistakes as part of growth. She ignores this, humiliates students who don't know everything, dismisses minority voices, and never offers guidance—making lectures hostile instead of supportive

Loved Palmer. Wise, inspirational professor.

Prof. Palmer led our class to be the most authentic version of a Socratic seminar I have experienced within my education. Often shared his decades of lived experience fighting for racial justice in a way that genuinely entwined with our course material. Some of the most engaging, critical discussions I've ever had.
Learning from Dr. Palmer was transformative and truly fulfilling. He fosters community and brave and honest discussions.

Professor Palmer is a great person and truly works for the personal liberation of his students. There are some frustrating aspects of being in his classroom, but ultimately the learning experience trumps those challenges. Don't take his class if you're not willing to openly discuss your own racial background.

Palmer's intelligence, passion, and articulation of complex social justice ideas are very striking.  It is fascinating to hear him speak.  You must do the reading, because he will assign mini-presentations of readings at the last minute, which is annoying-but worth it. Prof Palmer is extremely passionate about teaching young people and making a difference in society.  He is an excellent role model and lives his life by his teachings.  His class is inspiring.  Buy the books and be prepared to do the readings!
Smart, passionate, and if he's on your side, he is definitely on your side.

Prof. Palmer is great. Sometimes he talks about himself too much, but otherwise the class is well worth it.
Professor Prashant Purohit is very nice responsive and supportive.

Dr. Davies is an incredible instructor he is the most engaging professor I have ever had the pleasure of taking a course with.
One of the best professors I have ever had, really cares about each student and wants everyone to succeed.

Davies was an amazing professor! his interactive powerpoints made class exiting and engaging. I liked his jokes each class and I could tell that he put a lot of thought and effort into the structure of his class each week. He was also very helpful during office hours and clarified any confusion that I had.

Dr. Davies is an incredible professor. He communicates the subject material in an instructive and entertaining manner and is committed to making sure every student is able to succeed in his class. He excelled in transferring the course to a virtual format.
Dr. Davies puts in so much time to make things interesting and engaging to students and makes class fun (even though it had to be on zoom), amazing instructor who genuinely cares for students.

Prof Davies is one of the most entertaining chem teachers I've had. He is passionate about the content and it spreads to the students. He also loves to interact with the students and makes jokes that are always funny. I wish I could have him for all my chem courses.
Dr. Davies teaches in a very clear way. He gets straight to the point, and explains the material in a way that makes practical sense. I would definitely take more classes taught my Dr. Davies.

Best professor I have had yet. Very understanding of the struggles of school, but also encourages people to try in his class. Cares so much about his students and pushes us to learn. I loved this class so much, can't believe I'll miss Thermo!
Took intro to materials with Professor Davies; course was awesome, entertaining and interesting. Highly recommended to anyone wanting to learn more about MSE. Great professor!
A great professor. I truly enjoyed my time and learned a lot of things!
Professor Davies is an amazing professor who truly wants students to learn; best teacher I've had in my major, if not at this entire university.
The best professor I've had at Penn so far--and one of the best teacher's I've ever had.
Davies is engaging, funny, organized and entertaining uses excellent examples to engage his students; one of the best teachers on campus.  Take his course if you get the chance.
I took this course last semester; I have never had a better teacher . Dr. Davies cares so much about his students: awesome
Took thermo this spring, Dr. Davies spent many extra hours helping me understand concepts.  Really cares about students, best professor I've had so far.
Really good notes and explanations; actually makes Thermodynamics understandable.  Entertaining professor who cares about students and lectures - best I've had this year.
Chemistry is not my favorite subject, but this professor is. He  makes things clear and connects to interesting examples in technology.  Dr. Davies is by far my best prof.
All these comments are true, great professor and funny.  Kept my interest in a course that was hard for me.
Amazing lecturer - relates basic chem to emerging technology, great at keeping our interest.
Unbelievable at making incomprehensible material understandable and clear.  Best prof in the school, funny too.
Great jokes, very funny.  Connects basic chem to real applications, love this professor
Sooo helpful in office hours and clear in lectures.  Of all my engineering profs he is the absolute best
Currently taking frosh chem; all comments are true.  Fantastic prof, really funny but jokes have a purpose - they help you learn.  Best of all my current teachers
These ratings are true - clarity to the max and very funny.
Amazing at explaining difficult concepts - thermo rocks when Davies is teaching it.
Funny very clear explanations - you've got to take one of his classes
Amazing engineering prof; connects what can be boring material to interesting applications.  Very funny - go to his lectures and sit at the front.
Just outstanding
Awesome prof!

best professor iv'e ever had at penn
She is a fair grader, while also providing useful feedback on assignments. Pam is awesome and worth taking again!

She is one of my favorite professors at this school. She makes the material interesting and engaging and I always looked forwards to her classes. She is very accommodating and understanding, and her assignments are not difficult.

Many Urdu speaking students take her class. If they are there to question, work, and improve, they do well. If they are there for the easy grade and don't take it or her seriously, this class will not suit them.

Marsha is the best professor I have ever had (and I have 4 degrees from 3 universities). SHE IS A REAL HUMAN BEING--Not the typical Ivy League arrogant instructor. Excellent teacher on so many levels.  Clear and organized thinking  while deeply insightful about the content.  Got a lot from taking her. Fave teacher at Penn hands down. (senior now)

she has an underlying expectation that you must know everything as if we are not students .

Professors like her are why people say Penns MSW doesn't live up to the hype .  Nothing about her is extraordinary, but she will act as if everything that comes out of her mouth is golden. It's outdated and not evidenced based on. She's very woo-woo…
Literally take anyone but her!  You will learn so much more and have much less stress…

Want to ruin your GPA and your confidence at the same time? Sign up. I was a stellar student before her class but she ruined me . I'm now applying to law school  to advocate against the many women like her who get into social work for power and control .

Feedback is minimal or unhelpful, and she seems more focused on appearing knowledgeable than actually supporting student growth. Time in her class feels wasted, not educational.

Sarah is a truly exceptional professor. She's brilliant, funny and brings the material to life in a way that will stay with me for years to come. As a respected psychotherapist, her expertise is invaluable for clinical students. She's also incredibly generous with her time and genuinely cares about preparing the next generation of social workers.

The issue is not rigorous writing expectations but the overwhelming weight placed on them. Feedback rarely reflects actual knowledge or effort. The course lacks clear rubrics, creating a grading system that feels opaque, inconsistent, and vulnerable to bias. Formal criteria are urgently needed for fairness.
This “professor” had soo many more negative reviews on here . classmates said that when they wrote negative reviews , the  reviews were reported and removed . Same thing happened to me.

Her grading process was frustrating. Despite significant effort, she  was fixated on minor details of writing style, and the lack of any official rubrics made my final grade feel like a complete personal judgment call. It was clear that my performance was judged unfairly, resulting in a grade that did not reflect my understanding of the material.

She presents herself as an expert, but her cold, elitist demeanor and obsession with abstract psychological theory make her utterly incapable of providing anything of real-world value. reviews.comment: Sarah's classes are by far the best ones I have taken at SP2. She is brilliant, thoughtful, funny, and shares a wealth of invaluable clinical knowledge. Doesn't take a rocket scientist to tell when someone writes their own reviews… Don't take this class if you want your actual thoughts and ideas critiqued. Even perfectly fine writing gets ripped to shreds, and the feedback focuses more on how to write like she wants rather than engaging with your work.
Amazing professor and remarkable human being. Absolutely made me a more a critical thinker and contributed significantly to any success I achieve as a social worker. Take her class.

One tiny mistake can turn a perfectly written paper into something “unacceptable” and brand you a poor writer.She slashes points for the smallest writing issues until your whole grade collapses. She grades as if we're all English majors instead of social work students and demands flawless,doctoral-level writing instead of master's level work.Awful.

Doc Trots is literally the best prof at SP2 but she makes you work hard. She's a perfectionist herself so she expects us to turn in good papers and think hard about becoming therapists. Sarah goes above & beyond. Regardless of you being clinical or macro, I highly recommend taking her courses. She is brilliant & speaks using personal experience that help students relate to the material. She is by far one of the best professors at Penn in regards to not only her lessons but also her ability to know each student in a meaningful way.

Sarah is one of the most caring professors at SP2. She takes time out of her day to meet with students to talk about anything from class, to how field is going, or whatever you need to talk about. She takes the time to get to know each student & gives detailed & thoughtful feedback on assignments. By far one of the best professors I have ever had.

Sarah Trotta is a great professor who will teach you applicable skills for the field and new ways to analyze mental health. She is very accessible outside of class and will happily discuss your field work with you. She cares deeply about social work, her clients, and her students. I wish I had the opportunity to take another class with her.

Dr. Trotta has a way with words. She makes complex concepts simple and digestable.

great teacher - take his class even if you're a non-major

Really enjoyed working on my independent studies with him. Excellent experience.

Worst prof. Countless assignments each week, at the end of the semester, you'll finish 21++ assignment. He loves using mathematics prove concept, but the proving progress is extremely not serious.  I mean he let two obvious different variables as the same, ridiculous. He won't curve, and grade tough. Assigs have logic issues but due won't be delay.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: One of the best profs ever!
Awesome prof

If there is such a title as a cyber samurai this prof is it. He is a far cry from all the other profs who just preach. This prof does a lot of deep show and tell. He is also a real internetwork expert and cyber hacker. He recommends key books but honestly he should be teaching the guys who write the books. He is an expert++ and demands precision.

Hardest class I have taken. You need to know everything - servers, routers, switches, vmware, programming, bits, bytes. Prof knows all this inside and out - has real world practical experience. You will get no credit for something that sort of works - class demands very high level of precision and perfection to become a cyber kung fu master.

For professor the network either works or not - very binary 1 or 0. No partial credit after so much work. Always available to help understand concepts but you need to put in lots lots  of effort and show you want to learn. All classes hands on and  shows you so many tricks. Part of DoD cyber warrior program - pretty serious stuff.
clear on assignments and easy on grading.  easy work load for the week.

Professor is way passionate about security and really knows it inside out. His eyes and red lips get way intense when he is so focused on topic. Prof is always online 2am, 5am, 10pm - doesn't matter he can do a conference call anytime. He needs perfection so you need make sure you learn the material and core concepts.

Very very hard to get an A in this class. Instructor is always helpful and I always get response in less that 5 minutes. Instructor is a real cyber and internet expert - he teaches but also does detail show in class. No credit for attendance like other classes but if you want to learn you need to show up or you lose a lot.

The professor is quite open to discussion and  the class shows how to use the security tricks. He is not big on deadlines but be careful - if you don't do the work in time you will be lost on the next week's homework. Make sure to submit all papers before last day of class and you are good. Don't expect to get any credit for showing up to class.

Several points:
1. His voice is too low, handwriting is unreadable.
2. The course is unreasonably fast. It is impossible to do most of the proofs carefully with this speed. Though big picture is important, even people previously saw the topic before couldn't follow up is too much.
3. Too many typos and even dumb mistakes in the homework and exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Takes time to provide useful examples
No Comments
The subject matter isn't easy, but Ziller did a good job of taking the time to explain anything we didn't understand.

Funny Russian accent and cool guy.  Great examples in physics for calculus problems.  Found lectures really interesting using MAPLE programs in class.  Very helpful in office hours and was available outside of class for questions.  Really nice math prof.
Avoid her if u can

As a virtual educator (due to pandemic), Witmer tries her best to maintain an in-classroom atmosphere. Her exams are pretty difficult, but it's not impossible to do well. Her cell biology class aligns well with MCAT Biochemistry. She answers questions matter-of-factly; don't take offense to it!

If you can avoid taking class with her, do it. She is not kind or understanding. Once told a student she should reconsider medical school for raising her hand to ask a question. Stating that she felt her lectures are easily understood and therefore there should be no questions.

Part of the problem was her incredibly thick accent, but overall I had a terrible experience and had to withdraw after only missing a few lectures due to a severe case of bronchitis. She also constantly referred to her own class as a pre med weed out class, which isnt something professors should do imo. She was very condescending and discouraging.
I have never seen a professor as devoted, patient, and har-working as her!!!
She genuinely cares about her students and would stay much longer after every class to answer questions as well as provide practice problems.
Great professor. if u want clear answers for ur questions from her, u can ask her after class
Aside from being ridiculously out of sync with the textbook and the English language, she was cute. That is all.

Some issues in clarity, but not so much that you can't figure it out if you try. She is definitely not going to spoon-feed anyone anything, but she is very invested and if you follow her guidelines, the class is significantly more manageable.

Undoubtedly, THE WORST educator I have ever had in my entire life. Doesn't speak english--or write tests in understandable english. Has favorites. She even talked about how much she hates her boss. AVOID at all costs...it's worth the money to switch to the day class if you're in LPS.

the worst!!                                       moderate to severe language difficulty, deliberately unavailable TA's, poor communication,  lab ahead of lecture, blackboard not utilized, exam problem questions do not match lectures, unable to "teach" material and uninterested in changing to improve, responds to favorite students

Thick accent, difficult to understand, horrible tests with many typos and problem sets are often outside the scope of the course. As a person she's friendly, interested in her field and easy to catch during office hours/after class etc, however she's close to the top of my list of 'worst professors I've ever had.'

She is not bad. True, there are issues with exams (typos and clarity), however, she is fully invested in her students and interested in their success.  Gives weekly MCAT quizzes relevant to current material, which is extremely helpful for MCAT prep. Overall, you will learn what you need to, and she is definitely knowledgeable.. just be patient

She's far from the worst. She's generous with her time, is very nice, and loves the topic. Language is the biggest problem, as you'll often ask her a question, and she'll simply repeat what she said previously. Exams can be hard, but labs and quizzes are pretty easy. Be prepared to put in the yards and you'll do fine.
worst!

Unhelpful.  Prioritizes research above teaching. Thick accent, cannot convey lessons to students.  Text is wonderful but unhelpful for the purposes of doing well in her class. Makes tests up the night before, they are full of typos and difficult to understand.  Very disorganized.
She lies.  She is not helpful in anyway.  She will ruin your GPA.  Do not take her.
No Comments
She has a good heart but she is a horrible professor. I would discourage anyone from taking her class. It is very hard to understand her and she is very insecure about what she teaches.

Worst professor I've had the displeasure of taking classes with.  Worries more about finished lectures than if students understand.  Exams are horribly worded.  And worst of all, no one can understand a word she says.  Not being a Penn student, this course has discouranged me from taking other Penn courses in the future! F------------------------
Dr. Schur is such a great teacher. He puts so much effort into his classes and really does everything in his own time to help students succeed.
He reports you if you get in trouble
Mamash Geshmak

Dr. Schur is a virtual einstein. How Penn ever landed such a professor I'll never know. He deserves instant tenure. Taking his class is an experience that everyone should have at least once in their lifetime.
Wonderful class! I love Dr. Schur!!!
Good class! I adored Dr. Schur then, I still do, and always will!
Dr. Schur is a phenomenal professor. Great class!
Dr. Schur presented "Death in Medieval Judaism and Christianity" very well. I like how he did not require additional research for the final exercise. I would happily take this class again!
Excellent class!

Dr. Schur is the type of professor you expect to encounter at an ivy league university. He is professional, efficient and has an acute attention to detail. Dr. Schur is friendly without being a people pleaser and an eloquent speaker with no trace of arrogance. Exceptional instructors and intellectuals like Dr. Schur are seldom found in academia.
Read the textbook thoroughly and you will do well on his exams. Also don't wait last minute to start your projects do small amounts over the coarse of the semester.

The course on International Trade is a high level course at the Graduate Center. Dr. S. is a kind, genial gentleman, who has worked in International Trade for many years and is an icon in the field. The works involves heavy analysis and presentations of research papers which grants the freedom to learn on your own, and class powerpoints on theory.

Totally disrespectful towards students. It was not diffucult to get the grade,however the course was quite intense, carried out in a short period of timeActually, the course was useless as the professor did not provide us with any knowledge-not only the presentation was not his, but also he just read the slides which we could do on our own at home
I so much regret taking his course. All I have learned about Public Finance I learned from the textbook. Making college students read articles aloud in class is nonsense.
I took one class from this loser this last semester as a junior and it stunk.  I got an A grade but the class was sub-standard in every way.  Very befuddled uninsightful instructor.

This class was a total waste of time because the professor was so bad.  He hardly understands what he is even teaching.  He does not have the skill to teach at the college level, let alone a school such as Penn.  He would be much better suited to teach junior high school, maybe high school.  Do not take his classes, they are horrible.

Absolutly incompetent professor and a total disgrace to Penn.  Class was absolutely atrocious. exams tested on material not even alluded to in class.  Terribly boring lecturer.  Many students did not even bother attending lectures because they were ultimately a waste of time.
This class was, at its very best, slightly below mediocre.  I would not recommend this class to majors or non-majors.

I have somehow ended up taking three classes from him, and he is by far the worst teacher I've ever had. He makes generally interesting subjects seem duller and can't teach his subjects at all- you are better off studying independently.
great teacher. i was personally very motivated by what i learned in his class.

He is the worst professor ever.  He insults his students, presses his political views on them, and tests his class on material not covered (or even in the textbook for that matter).  He was teaching a course in development...but instead we learned about outdated international  trade models and about how much he hates Barack Obama.
this guy was horrible - had him 10+ years ago...
Obnoxious, condescending
Gross!!  What is he doing teaching here??  I pay for this monkey to teach me.
Awesome teach.
I heart Yochanan.  What more is there to say?  He's the reason I majored in Econ.
No Comments
He was funny, but I would go ahead and call him a terrible teacher.  I learned -next- to nothing from him.  My TA, on the other hand, was outstanding.
He completely ignored the syllabus and couldn't teach his way out of a paper bag.
obnoxious, condescending, and unhelpful;  but importantly, he knows his stuff
Incredibly funny, but a bit obnoxious, and not everyone likes his lecture style
HE WILL COME BACK TO THE WORLD AS A DOG IN THE NEXT GILGUL

Exceptionally brilliant. Always affable and willing to answer any questions any time. Has a unique insight into things that are based on mathematics, philosophical insight and a deep appreciation for the complexity of life. Unique teaching style which I enjoy.

Ziv is probably the best professor I've ver had. He was able to teach a non quant student some of the most quant heavy complicated material I have ever encountered. He's a true gem and you should definitely take the opportunity to take any class with him if you can. To boot, truly cares about his students and them learning the material! 110+% Ziv!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

reviews.comment: Goes beyond the mechanics of the subject. Gives you lot of insights about real world applications.  students should be very active and focused in class to get the lecture fully. Best prof i had here at ISB.

Amazing perspective on decision making  under uncertainty. Goes beyond the ask of the course to develop an intuitive understanding of the concepts and their applications in students. While you will understand the real world applications and will be left in awe of the subject, the lectures do not equip you completely to take the tests.

Dr. Z is a nice man, but pretty bad at presenting information. He teaches at a level well above undergraduate understanding, and it requires a lot of outside resources to comprehend the material. His lectures are disorganized and vague, I'm basically teaching myself the course. Quizzes are poorly worded and confusing, homework is not much better.

Really sweet guy, genuinely wants everyone to succeed and learn. He can be a bit all over the place in lecture, but his past exams (which he makes available to current students) are very indicative of what you'll be asked. Love the drop-one-test option! He is a genetics guy, so expect the material to be challenging. Record & go over lectures!
very nice guy
Hilarious, engaging lectures  for those of us not already really into archaeology, the subject can get a bit dry, but he manages to spice it up with great anecdotes from back in the day.
reviews.rating_tags: Tests? Not many--Hilarious--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Not very together, but informative and engaging. A true anthropology department bred oddball. It would have been nice to be able to have purchasable hard copies of the dense readings or a better classroom standard of expectation, but then I may be just the person to which he's referring with the quip "is anal-retentive hyphenated?"
Clueless as to what the students really need to get out of the class. The class taught is not the class tested. Independent study might have been a better alternative.
Course material/classes have nothing to do with the midterm or papers, but he has lots of great slides and stories about wet sheep and overturned vehicles and stuff
What a champ. Wish he was my dad.

HOW COULD ANYONE GIVE BIG Z A POOR RATING???  Philosophy of Space & Time was a cool class and Zoltan was hilarious.  Class isn't hard at all either, though there is a bunch of annoying memorization.  Interesting material though.
Excellent prof with a great sense of humor, he takes great interest in his students

I loved this class.  Prof. Domotor is very funny, actually, and if you get logic, the course is a real breath of fresh air.  I took it as an elective, and I couldn't bear to drop it, so I sacrificed an English credit for it!
Extremely clear.
